# [clang] Assertion `0 && "Invalid SLocOffset or bad function choice"' failed.

**Author:** yijan4845
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/115007

## Body

**This testcase is generated by a fuzzer.**

Compiler Explorer: [https://godbolt.org/z/Tfv7bvTdr](https://godbolt.org/z/Tfv7bvTdr)

This invalid code will crash on Clang Assertion Trunk.
```cpp
class Foo {
public:
  Foo(int);
  bool operator==(const int l);
};
#define EQ(x,y) (void)(x == y)  // expected-note 6{{defined here}}
void test_EQ() {
  Foo F = Foo{1};
  EQ(F,Foo{1,2});
}
```

It seems that this goes back to clang-12. The stack dump is very similar to [21755](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/21755).

Stack dump:
```
clang++: /root/llvm-project/clang/lib/Basic/SourceManager.cpp:866: clang::FileID clang::SourceManager::getFileIDLoaded(clang::SourceLocation::UIntTy) const: Assertion `0 && "Invalid SLocOffset or bad function choice"' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace, preprocessed source, and associated run script.
Stack dump:
0.	Program arguments: /opt/compiler-explorer/clang-assertions-trunk/bin/clang++ -gdwarf-4 -g -o /app/output.s -mllvm --x86-asm-syntax=intel -fno-verbose-asm -S --gcc-toolchain=/opt/compiler-explorer/gcc-snapshot -fcolor-diagnostics -fno-crash-diagnostics <source>
```

### Comment 2 - erichkeane

Found this and debugged for a few moments.  I don't understand source-manager much at all (@hokein has been poking at it lately:) ), BUT:

One thing I found is that in `getFileIDSlow` that `SLocOffset` was exactly equal to `NextLocalOffset`.  IF I changed the condition and relaxed the assert in `getFileIDLocal` the above seems to work perfectly.

This makes me think this is an off-by-1-error somewhere, and perhaps could use someone who knows BETTER than me to confirm this.  Either we're producing a source-location that we shouldn't be, forgetting to update the local-offsets, or something like that?  The `CurrentLoadedOffset` is its 'default' value of `2147483648` during my debug of this.

```
ekeane@dev-epyc4:/local/home/ekeane/llvm-project/clang$ git diff
diff --git a/clang/lib/Basic/SourceManager.cpp b/clang/lib/Basic/SourceManager.cpp
index b2b1488f9dc8..a7009916e7eb 100644
--- a/clang/lib/Basic/SourceManager.cpp
+++ b/clang/lib/Basic/SourceManager.cpp
@@ -799,7 +799,7 @@ FileID SourceManager::getFileIDSlow(SourceLocation::UIntTy SLocOffset) const {
 
   // Now it is time to search for the correct file. See where the SLocOffset
   // sits in the global view and consult local or loaded buffers for it.
-  if (SLocOffset < NextLocalOffset)
+  if (SLocOffset <= NextLocalOffset)
     return getFileIDLocal(SLocOffset);
   return getFileIDLoaded(SLocOffset);
 }
@@ -809,7 +809,7 @@ FileID SourceManager::getFileIDSlow(SourceLocation::UIntTy SLocOffset) const {
 /// This function knows that the SourceLocation is in a local buffer, not a
 /// loaded one.
 FileID SourceManager::getFileIDLocal(SourceLocation::UIntTy SLocOffset) const {
-  assert(SLocOffset < NextLocalOffset && "Bad function choice");
+  assert(SLocOffset <= NextLocalOffset && "Bad function choice");
   assert(SLocOffset >= LocalSLocEntryTable[0].getOffset() && SLocOffset > 0 &&
          "Invalid SLocOffset");
   assert(LocalSLocEntryTable.size() == LocalLocOffsetTable.size());
ekeane@dev-epyc4:/local/home/ekeane/llvm-project/clang$ 
```

---

### Comment 3 - hokein

Ah, an off-by-one error — I think I’ve run into similar issues before. This is caused by the lexer's error recovery...

I guess (didn't look at it deeply) what happens in this case:

- the lexer inserts parentheses `()` around the macro argument `Foo{1, 2}`, so the token stream becomes:
`(`, `Foo`, `{`, `1`, `,`, `2`, `}`, `)`
- however, the file ID size for the macro argument only accounts for the tokens that were actually written:
`Foo`, `{`, `1`, `,`, `2`, `}`

The extra inserted `)` token points to the end of the range which is the `NextLocalOffset`;



---
